zh.St. Peter, Leeds, Yorkshire, England: 英國英格蘭越可爾利德斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Bywell於1593年出生在英國越可爾利德斯時，她的父親Richard Bywell34歲，母親Lucy Greene34歲。她於1614年7月12日與Francis Lister在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1647年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦享年54歲，埋葬在英國越可爾吉斯本。

nameMeanings.name: Bywell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name, toponymic from Bywell (Northumb), or topographic for someone who lived ‘(by the) stream’, Middle English bi, by ‘by, next’ + wel(le) ‘spring, stream’ (Old English bī + wiella).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Bywell was born in 1593, in Leeds, Yorkshire, England, her father, Richard Bywell, was 34 and her mother, Lucy Greene, was 34. She married Francis Lister on 12 July 1614, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1647, in New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 54, and was buried in Gisburn, Yorkshire, England.

fr.New London, Connecticut Colony, British: New London, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Bywell est née en 1593, à Leeds, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Richard Bywell, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Lucy Greene, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Francis Lister le 12 juillet 1614, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1647, à New London, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterrée à Gisburn, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.New London, Connecticut Colony, British: New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America
de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Bywell 1593 in Leeds, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Bywell 34 und ihre Mutter Lucy Greene 34. Sie hat am 12. Juli 1614 in Yorkshire, England Francis Lister geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1647 im Alter von 54 Jahren in New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Gisburn, Yorkshire, England begraben.

Lei sposato Francis Lister il 12 luglio 1614, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1647, nel New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolta a Gisburn, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Bywellが1593年にイギリスヨークシュアイアリーズで生まれた時，父Richard Bywellは34歳，母Lucy Greeneは34歳であった。1614年7月12日にイギリスヨークシュアイアでFrancis Listerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1647年にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ロンドンで54歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアギスブルンにて埋葬された。

ko.New London, Connecticut Colony, British: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 런던
ko.lifeSummary: Jane Bywell(이)가 1593년 영국 요르크실르 리드즈에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Bywell(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lucy Greene(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1614년 7월 12일 영국 요르크실르에서 Francis Lister(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1647년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 런던에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 지스번에 묻혔다.

pt.New London, Connecticut Colony, British: New London, Conecticute, British Colonial America
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Bywell nasceu em 1593, em Leeds, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Bywell, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Lucy Greene, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com Francis Lister em 12 de julho de 1614, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1647, em New London, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 54 anos, e foi sepultada em Gisburn, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.New London, Connecticut Colony, British: ню лондон, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Bywell родилась в 1593 г. в местности лидс, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Bywell было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Lucy Greene, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Francis Lister 12 июля 1614 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1647 г. в местности ню лондон, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 54 лет и была похоронена в местности гисбурн, еркшир, Англия.
